title: Gerascanthus ochnaceus
taxonomy: Gerascanthus ochnaceus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It belongs to the genus Gerascanthus, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the family Celastraceae, Gerascanthus ochnaceus is placed in the order Celastrales, which consists of various other flowering plants. The Celastrales order is part of the clade Rosidae, a larger group of flowering plants. This classification helps to group and identify Gerascanthus ochnaceus within the plant kingdom.

intro: Gerascanthus ochnaceus is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Gerascanthus ochnaceus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gerascanthus ochnaceus is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
